title: Tone Marker × Archetype Integration
description: Models how each genre's Tone Marker interacts with each archetype's emotional arc. Each pairing is classified as reinforcing (tone amplifies the arc's natural trajectory), contrasting (tone works against the arc, creating productive dissonance), or neutral (tone and arc operate independently). Based on emotional arc profiles from archetype_emotional_arcs.json.

archetype_interactions.note: The Hero's Journey's escalating tension through trials and ordeal naturally supports sustained seriousness.
The social stakes and crisis of worth carry genuine dramatic weight.
Quests can be serious but the adventure elements can pull toward lighter tones.
The fascination phase tends toward wonder rather than seriousness.
The escalating threat and nightmare phase demand serious treatment.
Moral reckoning and atonement are inherently serious.
Tragedy is the most naturally serious archetype — the descending arc demands gravitas.
Comedy's playful chaos fundamentally resists sustained seriousness.
Loss of innocence and identity crisis carry genuine emotional weight.
The sustained high tension and moral cost of revenge demand serious treatment.
Confinement and the stakes of the escape attempt are inherently serious.
The decision to sacrifice carries maximum dramatic seriousness.
Mystery investigation can be serious or playful depending on stakes.
Identity dissolution and reformation are deeply serious processes.
The trajectory from peak to collapse demands sustained seriousness.
The Hero's Journey's progression through trials and ordeal maps directly to escalating action sequences.
The rise can be kinetic but the crisis of worth requires emotional stillness.
The Quest's journey structure naturally generates kinetic forward momentum.
The escape from the other world provides natural kinetic climax.
Monster confrontation is pure kinetic energy in its ideal form.
Rebirth requires interior stillness and reflection that opposes kinetic momentum.
Tragedy's descending arc resists forward momentum — the protagonist is being pulled down, not propelled forward.
Comic chaos can be kinetic but the energy is chaotic rather than directional.
Psychological maturation requires pacing and reflection that kinetic energy disrupts.
The pursuit and escalation phases are naturally high-energy.
The escape attempt is pure kinetic energy — maximum forward momentum.
The decision and sacrificial act require emotional weight that kinetic energy can undercut.
Investigation requires patience and thought that opposes kinetic momentum.
Identity change is interior and requires stillness.
The ascent can be kinetic but the fall requires deceleration.
The Hero's Journey's serious ordeal resists playfulness, though lighter versions exist.
Social mobility comedy works when the rise is charming rather than grim.
Comic quests work when the companions and obstacles are absurd.
The fascination phase and fish-out-of-water dynamics naturally generate playfulness.
Genuine threat is hard to maintain in a playful tone.
Comic rebirth works (e.g., Groundhog Day) but the atonement must carry real weight.
Playfulness fundamentally opposes tragic gravity — the combination creates dark comedy or satire.
Perfect alignment — the comedy archetype IS the structural expression of playful tone.
Adolescent awkwardness and social miscalibration naturally generate playful comedy.
The sustained rage and moral gravity of revenge resist playfulness.
Comic escapes work but the stakes must still feel real.
Sacrifice demands emotional weight that playfulness undercuts.
Comic mysteries (e.g., Clue) work when the investigation is charming.
Comic transformations work when the liminal state is played for humor.
The fall portion resists playful treatment unless the fall itself is comic.
The ordeal phase aligns with dread, but hope-driven phases resist it.
The hopeful ascent resists sustained dread.
Dread can infuse the final approach but not the team-building phases.
The shadow emergence and frustration phases align perfectly with mounting dread.
The nightmare phase IS sustained dread in its purest form.
The fallen state carries dread but the renewal arc resolves it.
Tragedy's inevitable descent aligns with sustained dread — the audience knows it's coming.
Comedy's promise of recoverable harm directly opposes dread's uncertainty.
The identity crisis can carry dread but most of the arc doesn't.
The sustained high tension and moral uncertainty of revenge generate natural dread.
Confinement and fear of discovery generate sustained dread.
The approaching inevitable sacrifice generates mounting dread.
The deepening mystery and approaching truth generate intellectual dread.
Identity dissolution generates existential dread.
The cracks forming and desperate grip phases are pure dread.
The special world entry and mentor phase thrive on wonder; the mythic scale matches grandeur.
Wonder can enhance the magical transformation but the social mechanics may be mundane.
The journey through varied landscapes is a natural vehicle for sustained wonder.
The fascination phase IS wonder in its purest expression.
Mythic monsters and heroic confrontation carry natural grandeur.
Rebirth's interior moral focus doesn't naturally require wonder.
Wonder resists the crushing inevitability of tragedy — grandeur can coexist but wonder cannot.
Comic fantasy exists but wonder and humor operate independently.
A young person's first encounters with magical worlds generate powerful wonder.
Revenge's focused fury opposes wonder's expansive openness.
The escape can pass through wondrous settings but the urgency resists contemplation.
Grandeur can enhance the sacrifice's scale but wonder is not the dominant emotion.
Fantasy mysteries exist but the investigative mode resists pure wonder.
Magical transformation and wonder are naturally aligned.
The rise can be wondrous but the fall is not.
The Hero's Journey is primarily emotional; intellectual engagement is secondary.
Social ascent can be intellectualized but doesn't require it.
The quest can pursue intellectual goals but the structure is adventure-driven.
Exploring alien worlds naturally generates intellectual curiosity.
Understanding the monster's nature can be intellectual but the climax is physical.
Rebirth is moral rather than intellectual.
Intellectual engagement enhances the audience's understanding of why the tragedy was inevitable.
Intellectual comedy exists but the mode is wit rather than curiosity.
Coming of age is primarily emotional and social.
Revenge is emotionally driven; intellectual analysis can dilute the urgency.
Planning an escape is an intellectually engaging process.
The sacrifice is emotional rather than intellectual.
Investigation IS intellectual engagement — the two are perfectly aligned.
Transformation is experiential rather than intellectual.
The arc can be analyzed intellectually but doesn't require intellectual tone.
The Hero's Journey's trials and special world exploration generate natural excitement.
The rise can be exciting but the social dynamics may not be.
The Quest IS adventure — journey, obstacles, and discovery at every turn.
Departure into unknown territory is the essence of adventure excitement.
Monster hunting generates excitement through escalating encounters.
Rebirth's interior moral journey resists the externalized excitement of adventure.
Tragedy's inevitability opposes adventure's open-ended excitement.
Comic adventures exist but the energy source differs.
First experiences and discovery align naturally with coming-of-age.
The pursuit can be exciting but revenge's darkness resists pure adventure tone.
The escape attempt generates natural excitement and discovery.
Sacrifice requires solemnity that opposes adventure excitement.
Discovery in mystery is intellectual rather than physical.
Transformation is interior rather than externally exciting.
The ascent can be exciting but the fall is not adventurous.
The Hero's Journey can carry romantic intensity when the ordeal is emotional.
Romantic Cinderella stories thrive on emotional intensity during the rise and crisis.
The Quest's external goal focus resists inward-directed emotional intensity.
The fascination phase can carry romantic intensity but the structure is broader.
Physical confrontation resists romantic atmosphere.
Redemption through love is emotionally intense by nature.
Tragic romance amplifies emotional intensity to its maximum.
Romantic comedy thrives on the tension between emotional intensity and comic playfulness.
First love and romantic awakening carry natural emotional intensity.
Revenge's fury opposes romantic tenderness.
Escape can be motivated by love but the tone is urgency rather than romance.
Sacrifice for a loved one is peak romantic emotional intensity.
Investigation's analytical mode resists romantic emotional intensity.
Transformation through love carries natural emotional intensity.
The arc can be romantic but doesn't require it.
The Hero's Journey's serious ordeals resist wit and playfulness.
Charming social ascent through romantic misadventure is a natural romcom vehicle.
Romantic quests can be witty but the structure pulls toward adventure.
Fish-out-of-water romantic scenarios generate natural wit and warmth.
Monster confrontation resists playful warmth.
Comic rebirth through romance works but atonement requires sincerity.
Tragedy fundamentally opposes the romcom promise of happy resolution.
Comedy and romcom share structural DNA — escalating chaos to reconciliation.
Adolescent romantic fumbling generates natural wit and warmth.
Revenge's darkness opposes romcom's essential warmth.
Escape from a bad relationship can be played for comedy.
Sacrifice's solemnity resists playful energy.
Romantic mystery comedy exists but the modes can clash.
Transformation through romantic comedy works when the change is charming.
The fall resists warmth and wit.
The ordeal can carry dread but the hopeful resolution resists sustained atmosphere.
Hopeful ascent fundamentally opposes sustained dread.
Adventure-quest energy opposes dread's suffocating atmosphere.
The other world becoming hostile generates perfect dread atmosphere.
Horror IS monster fiction's native genre — dread amplifies every phase.
The fallen state can be dreadful but renewal dissolves it.
Tragedy's descending arc naturally aligns with sustained dread.
Comic restoration actively fights dread atmosphere.
Horror coming-of-age exists but dread conflicts with growth's hope.
Revenge horror uses dread to intensify both the wrong and the pursuit.
Confinement plus dread is horror's most effective combination.
The approaching sacrifice under dread atmosphere creates maximum emotional impact.
Horror mystery uses dread to make each revelation more terrifying.
Body horror and identity dissolution generate existential dread.
The rise resists dread; the fall can carry it.
The Hero's Journey is emotional rather than intellectual.
Social ascent doesn't naturally generate intellectual curiosity.
The quest for knowledge aligns but physical quests don't.
Exploration generates curiosity but of a different kind.
Understanding the monster's weakness can be intellectual.
Rebirth is moral rather than intellectual.
Tragic irony engages the intellect but the arc is emotional.
Wit engages intellectually but the mode is humor.
Maturation is experiential rather than intellectual.
Revenge is driven by emotion, not curiosity.
Planning an escape requires intellectual engagement.
Sacrifice is emotional rather than intellectual.
Perfect alignment — the archetype IS intellectual investigation.
Transformation is experiential rather than analytical.
The arc can be analyzed but doesn't generate curiosity.
The Hero's Journey can carry moral weight when the ordeal is morally complex.
Criminal ascent carries inherent moral weight about the cost of success.
Quests are typically moral but not gritty.
The other world can be criminal but the structure isn't inherently moral.
The monster can represent moral evil but the fight is physical.
Criminal redemption carries heavy moral weight.
Crime tragedy is morally saturated — every choice carries consequence.
Comedy's lightness resists the gritty moral weight of crime.
Criminal coming-of-age exists but the innocence phase resists grittiness.
Criminal revenge carries extreme moral weight about justice vs. vigilantism.
Escape from criminal confinement carries moral weight about justice.
Criminal sacrifice carries moral weight about redemption through death.
Criminal investigation inherently explores moral questions.
Criminal transformation can carry moral weight but isn't inherently aligned.
Criminal rise and fall is saturated with moral judgment.
The Hero's Journey is intuitive and emotional; detective work is methodical.
Social ascent is not an intellectual exercise.
A quest for truth can be methodical but most quests are physical.
Exploration is experiential rather than methodical.
Understanding the monster can require rigor, but the climax is physical.
Rebirth is moral and emotional, not methodical.
Tragic irony rewards rigorous analysis but the arc is emotional.
Comedy's chaotic energy resists methodical rigor.
Maturation is experiential, not methodical.
Methodical revenge planning aligns but the emotional drive resists rigor.
Methodical escape planning aligns naturally with intellectual rigor.
Sacrifice is emotional, not intellectual.
Perfect alignment — methodical investigation IS the Mystery Unveiled archetype.
Transformation is experiential, not analytical.
Can be analyzed rigorously but doesn't require it.

integrations.tone_description: Heroic Grandeur Mixed with Personal Vulnerability

archetype_interactions.note: The Hero's Journey IS the superhero origin story structure — grandeur and vulnerability in perfect balance.
The rise from nobody to hero embodies heroic grandeur.
Heroic quests naturally carry grandeur.
The other world can be grand but vulnerability requires personal stakes.
The climactic battle against a supervillain is peak heroic grandeur.
Superhero redemption arcs carry grandeur but the tone shifts toward vulnerability.
Tragic fall opposes heroic grandeur — the combination is tragic heroism.
Comic superheroes exist but grandeur and comedy are in tension.
Young hero coming-of-age (Spider-Man) perfectly balances grandeur with vulnerability.
Heroic revenge can carry grandeur but risks losing moral clarity.
Heroic escape can be grand but vulnerability is dominant.
Heroic sacrifice is peak grandeur-vulnerability synthesis.
Investigation is methodical, not grand.
Superhero transformation carries natural grandeur with personal vulnerability.
The rise is grand but the fall subverts heroic tone.
Period atmosphere can enhance the special world but isn't structurally aligned.
Historical class mobility stories gain depth from period immersion.
Historical quests exist but the structure doesn't require period depth.
The other world AS a past era creates natural period immersion.
Historical monsters exist but period atmosphere is secondary.
Period setting can enhance rebirth but isn't required.
Historical tragedy gains weight from the period's constraints and values.
Period comedy exists but atmosphere can be light or heavy.
Growing up in a specific era gains depth from period immersion.
Historical revenge exists but period is backdrop.
Historical confinement (slavery, imprisonment) gains weight from period authenticity.
Historical sacrifice gains meaning from the period's values.
Historical mysteries exist but period is setting, not structure.
Period can enhance transformation but isn't structurally necessary.
Historical rise and fall gains gravitas from period constraints.
War hero's journey carries natural gravity through its trials and ordeal.
Military promotion is too narrow for full rags-to-riches structure.
Military missions as quests carry visceral intensity naturally.
War as an alien world that transforms you — the return is never to the same life.
The enemy as monster in war carries extreme gravity.
War-born redemption carries visceral intensity.
War tragedy is among the gravest narrative forms.
War comedy (M*A*S*H) uses humor as a contrasting survival mechanism against gravity.
Coming of age through war carries extreme intensity — innocence destroyed by violence.
War revenge carries visceral intensity.
POW escape carries maximum gravity and intensity.
Military sacrifice is the gravest form — laying down one's life for comrades.
War mysteries exist but the investigative mode resists visceral intensity.
War transforms identity with visceral intensity.
Military rise and fall carries natural gravity.
Biographical heroes exist but intimate gravity doesn't require mythic structure.
Biographical rise from obscurity carries intimate revelation of character.
Life quests can be biographical but the structure is adventure-oriented.
Biographical journeys exist but the other world framework is metaphorical.
Biographical struggles against adversity exist but lack monster-fighting structure.
Biographical redemption carries intimate gravity naturally.
Biographical tragedy gains power from the knowledge that it really happened.
Comic biography resists intimate gravity.
Biographical coming-of-age carries intimate revelation.
Biographical revenge exists but intimate gravity is secondary.
Biographical escape carries intimate gravity through real stakes.
Real sacrifice carries maximum intimate gravity.
Biographical mystery exists but investigation isn't intimate.
Biographical transformation carries intimate revelation.
Biographical rise and fall is historically grave.

integrations.tone_description: Warmth, Emotional Accessibility, and Earned Sentiment

archetype_interactions.note: Family hero stories can be warm but the ordeal must still carry weight.
Family-centered rags-to-riches stories thrive on warmth.
Family quests (road trips, reunions) carry natural warmth.
Family voyage and return carries warmth through homecoming.
Family vs. threat works but the monster-fighting reduces warmth.
Family-driven moral renewal carries natural warmth.
Family tragedy deeply opposes warmth — the combination creates devastating emotional impact.
Family comedy thrives on warmth and earned sentiment.
Family-supported coming-of-age carries natural warmth.
Revenge's darkness opposes family warmth.
Family escape can carry warmth but the urgency resists sentiment.
Parental sacrifice for family carries deep warmth through earned sentiment.
Family mysteries exist but investigation isn't inherently warm.
Family-driven transformation can be warm but isn't inherently so.
Family rise can be warm but the fall resists warmth.
YA hero's journey carries urgent emotional intensity through every phase.
Young person's rise carries intense emotional stakes.
YA quests carry emotional urgency.
Young person's first encounter with the wider world carries intense sincerity.
Young hero vs. monster carries emotional intensity through vulnerability.
Rebirth requires a fallen state that's unusual for young protagonists.
YA tragedy carries devastating emotional intensity.
YA comedy exists but intensity and humor can clash.
Coming of Age IS the YA archetype — emotional intensity and urgent sincerity define both.
YA revenge carries intensity but the moral complexity may be simplified.
YA escape carries intense emotional urgency.
Young person's sacrifice carries intense sincerity.
YA mystery exists but investigation resists urgent sincerity.
YA transformation carries emotional intensity through identity formation.
Rise and fall is unusual in YA unless it serves the coming-of-age arc.
The Hero's Journey's mythic structure resists contemplative interiority.
Literary social ascent can be contemplative but the structure pulls toward event.
The Quest's forward momentum resists contemplation.
Literary voyages can be contemplative but the structure demands movement.
Physical confrontation opposes contemplative precision.
Moral reckoning demands precisely the contemplative depth literary fiction provides.
Literary tragedy thrives on contemplative analysis of the protagonist's flaw.
Literary comedy exists but contemplation and comic timing can conflict.
Literary coming-of-age thrives on contemplative interiority.
Literary revenge can be contemplative but the drive resists stillness.
Escape's urgency opposes contemplative pacing.
The moral weight of sacrifice demands contemplative depth.
Literary mystery uses contemplation to deepen investigation.
Identity change is inherently contemplative territory.
Literary rise and fall gains depth from contemplative analysis of cause.
Children's hero journeys thrive on clear structure and warm wonder.
Fairy-tale rise stories carry natural warmth and clarity.
Children's quests are defined by imaginative energy.
The children's voyage to a magical land IS clarity + warmth + wonder.
Children's monster stories carry wonder through the monster's strangeness.
Children's moral renewal can carry warmth but the fallen state is unusual.
Tragedy opposes children's literature's promise of warmth and safety.
Children's comedy thrives on warmth and playful clarity.
Children's coming-of-age carries warmth through the growth journey.
Revenge's darkness opposes children's literature's warmth.
Children's escape can carry wonder but the stakes may be dark.
Children's sacrifice exists but the gravity conflicts with warmth.
Children's mystery generates imaginative energy through puzzle-solving.
Children's transformation carries wonder through magical change.
The fall opposes children's literature's essential warmth.
The Hero's Journey's earnest heroism is a natural target for satirical wit.
Social climbing can be satirized but the arc itself is earnest.
The Quest's earnest goal-pursuit is a natural satirical target (Don Quixote).
The other world as satirical mirror of our own is a classic structure.
Earnest monster-slaying is easily satirized.
Satirical rebirth can work when the moral failure being redeemed is social.
Satirical tragedy creates dark satire — the fall reveals systemic absurdity.
Satirical comedy uses wit to expose social absurdity through comic structure.
Satirical coming-of-age reveals social hypocrisy through young eyes.
Satirical revenge exposes justice systems but the earnest drive resists wit.
Satirical escape exposes institutional absurdity.
Sacrifice's earnest gravity opposes satirical wit.
Satirical investigation reveals institutional stupidity.
Satirical transformation reveals social construction of identity.
Rise and fall is a natural satirical structure — exposing the absurdity of ambition.

integrations.tone_description: Tone of Unsettling Interiority and Mounting Claustrophobia

archetype_interactions.note: The ordeal phase can be psychologically unsettling but the overall arc resists claustrophobia.
Social ascent is external rather than interior.
The Quest's expansive journey opposes claustrophobic interiority.
The other world can be psychologically unsettling.
The monster as psychological projection aligns but the physical confrontation doesn't.
The fallen state's interior darkness aligns with unsettling interiority.
Psychological tragedy IS unsettling interiority — the protagonist's mind is the battlefield.
Comedy's social externality opposes claustrophobic interiority.
Identity crisis can be psychologically unsettling but the overall arc resolves outward.
Revenge obsession creates mounting claustrophobic interiority.
Confinement creates literal and psychological claustrophobia.
The interior moral struggle can be unsettling but the act itself is external.
Psychological mystery creates unsettling interiority through unreliable perception.
Identity dissolution is maximum unsettling interiority.
The desperate grip phase can be psychologically claustrophobic.
The Hero's Journey can be stoic but the emotional arc demands more expression than sparse tone allows.
Social ascent requires social texture that spare tone resists.
Spare, elemental quests across frontier landscapes are the Western's core.
Frontier journey is spare but the fascination phase wants more texture.
The elemental confrontation — one hero vs. one threat — is peak stoic Western.
Western redemption is stoic by nature — the reformed gunslinger.
Western tragedy is spare and elemental — the fall is stark.
Comedy's playfulness opposes stoic spareness.
Western coming-of-age can be stoic but emotional growth requires expression.
Western revenge is the archetype of stoic, spare vengeance.
Frontier escape can be spare but the planning requires detail.
The stoic sacrifice without drama is a Western hallmark.
Investigation requires detail and analysis that spare tone resists.
Stoic transformation shows through action rather than interiority.
The arc can be spare but the social dynamics may need texture.

integrations.tone_description: Tone of Calculated Intelligence and Moral Ambiguity

archetype_interactions.note: The Hero's Journey's moral clarity opposes political ambiguity.
Political rise can be morally ambiguous.
The Quest's clear objective resists calculated ambiguity.
Political worlds can be morally ambiguous.
The clear hero-vs-monster dynamic resists moral ambiguity.
Political rebirth can be morally ambiguous — was the redemption strategic?
Political tragedy thrives on moral ambiguity — the fatal choice was calculated.
Political comedy can use ambiguity for ironic effect.
Political coming-of-age reveals moral ambiguity through loss of idealism.
Political revenge is calculated by nature — ambiguity about justice vs. power.
Political escape exists but the moral clarity of freedom resists ambiguity.
Political sacrifice may be calculated — was it altruism or strategy?
Political mystery thrives on calculated intelligence and ambiguous truth.
Political transformation may be strategic rather than genuine.
Political rise and fall is defined by calculated moves and moral ambiguity.
Mythic journey translates naturally to heightened musical expression.
Musical rise stories thrive on emotional expansion through song.
Musical quests use traveling songs and ensemble numbers.
The other world in a musical is inherently heightened.
Musical monster stories exist but physical confrontation can clash with performance.
Musical rebirth uses reprise transformation to make renewal performative.
Musical tragedy (Sweeney Todd, Les Misérables) uses heightened emotion to devastating effect.
Musical comedy is one of the most natural genre-archetype pairings.
Coming-of-age emotions are naturally heightened — musicals externalize what adolescents feel.
Musical revenge can work (Sweeney Todd) but the sustained fury is unusual for musicals.
Musical escape can be heightened but urgency resists performance breaks.
Musical sacrifice uses the showstopper to make the moment performative and devastating.
Investigation's analytical mode resists heightened performative expression.
Musical transformation uses reprise to make identity change audible.
Musical rise and fall uses ascending and descending melodies to match the arc.

integrations.tone_description: Warm, Nostalgic, Sentimental Tone with Earned Emotion

archetype_interactions.note: Holiday heroes exist but the mythic structure resists nostalgic warmth.
Holiday generosity transforming a poor person's life is a classic warm narrative.
Holiday quests (finding the perfect gift) can be warm but the structure is light.
Holiday return home carries deep nostalgia and warmth.
Monster confrontation opposes warm sentimentality.
Holiday moral rebirth (A Christmas Carol) is the most natural pairing — redemption through seasonal spirit.
Holiday tragedy deeply opposes the genre's warm promise.
Holiday comedy thrives on warm, nostalgic humor.
Holiday coming-of-age carries nostalgic warmth through seasonal tradition.
Revenge fundamentally opposes holiday warmth.
Escape's urgency opposes nostalgic sentiment.
Holiday sacrifice (Gift of the Magi) carries warm sentimentality.
Holiday mystery exists but investigation resists warmth.
Holiday transformation through seasonal spirit carries warm nostalgia.
The fall opposes holiday warmth.
